scope.signals: Existing replay-visible indications of convergence, stagnation, orbiting, novelty, process health, and verifier contact.
scope.interventions: Existing mechanisms that can alter attention, prompt context, model parameters, scheduling, ontology, evidence, or status.

prior_evidence_limits: The live basin study used one run per arm, one main problem, and few model families.
The historical reports are no longer present in the working tree and were inspected from commit eae535c.
The hashing embedder did not cleanly separate within-problem from cross-problem distances, so its absolute distances are not an evidence-mode convergence instrument.
The prior record is hypothesis-generating and does not calibrate prospective trigger probabilities or causal jolt effects.

signals.name: gate_block_count
signals.changes: none; reads Measure inputs beginning gate:
signals.calibration_history: Healthy basin arms recorded zero; two hard-orbit arms recorded 54 and 36 blocks.
signals.known_failure_modes: The existing implementation uses an event window rather than completed conjecturer opportunities and does not identify the blocked target, lineage, or family concentration.

signals.name: no_register_calls
signals.changes: none; record_llm_calls emits conj-noregister when a spent conjecturer call admits nothing
signals.calibration_history: Historical hard-orbit arms included 27 and 18 no-register cycles.
signals.known_failure_modes: A no-register call can result from malformed proposals, deduplication, or unrelated admission failures; it is not by itself evidence of orbiting.

signals.name: cycle_heartbeat
signals.changes: none; emits cycle number and selected problem id
signals.calibration_history: Used in prior basin reporting to identify empty cycles.
signals.known_failure_modes: A cycle without admission is not an empty conjecturer cycle when the selected path was pairwise, synthesis, no-problem, or transport-deferred.

signals.name: problem_age
signals.changes: none; derives prior conjectures on the same problem
signals.calibration_history: The basin report found novelty associated with problem age rather than global run length.
signals.known_failure_modes: Successor problems reset the identifier while retaining conceptual family age; raw problem-id age can understate carry-over.

signals.name: semantic_novelty
signals.changes: none; embeds admitted conjectures and computes distances
signals.replay_visible: artifact text is replay-visible; recomputed neural geometry is comparable only under an exact fingerprint
signals.calibration_history: Historical hashing geometry was poorly calibrated; a neural embedder install receipt exists for nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5.
signals.known_failure_modes: Raw distances are embedder-scale-specific, semantic distance can increase without functional change, and neural kernels can drift across environments.

signals.name: late_early_novelty_ratio
signals.changes: none; compares late and early within-run novelty
signals.calibration_history: Reported across prior basin arms, with fast stance decay and problem turnover showing the strongest recovery signals.
signals.known_failure_modes: Overlapping or tiny halves, a changing problem mix, and an unstable early baseline can make the ratio non-identifiable.

signals.name: school_convergence_geometry
signals.changes: none; computes inter-school centroid distance and a scale-normalised ratio
signals.calibration_history: The absolute 0.15 threshold was shown unsuitable on hashing geometry; the ratio path was added as a scale-normalised alternative.
signals.known_failure_modes: Sparse schools, unequal lineage size, and semantic convergence without functional stagnation create false positives.

signals.name: lineage_stagnation
signals.changes: none; combines negative semantic-distance slope with zero graph churn
signals.known_failure_modes: Graph churn is not verifier improvement, and semantic contraction can be healthy convergence.

signals.name: adjudication_ritual
signals.changes: none; combines attack entropy, criticism debt, reinstatement, and validity-node attack rate
signals.known_failure_modes: It diagnoses adjudication process, not generator convergence, and invoking its response can itself add deterministic attacks and problems.

signals.name: grounding_lambda
signals.changes: none; measures program/observation share or externally covered empirical claims
signals.enabled_by_default: spec lambda yes; stricter evidence lambda opt-in
signals.calibration_history: Evidence-lambda was added because program-heavy runs can peg the broader measure without external contact.
signals.known_failure_modes: The broad ratio credits internal well-formedness checks; the strict ratio is undefined for non-empirical tasks.

signals.name: stop_convergence
signals.replay_visible: final decision and metrics are logged; controller deque is reconstructable from cycle snapshots only when the same policy is retained
signals.calibration_history: Operational completion policy, not calibrated as a jolt trigger.
signals.known_failure_modes: Stable frontier/status/problem/admission counts do not establish absence of future verifier progress.

signals.name: controller_process_health
signals.changes: none; reads truncation and retry counts
signals.calibration_history: Bounded transport/process controller tests exist; it is intentionally blind to outcome metrics.
signals.known_failure_modes: Process health is a confound for jolt outcomes, not evidence of repertoire exhaustion.

signals.name: verifier_progress
signals.changes: execution-backed verdicts may create warrants, attacks, and status changes
signals.deterministic: yes for a frozen verifier/toolchain/input receipt
signals.replay_visible: verdict receipts are visible, but a common cross-domain progress delta is not currently emitted
signals.calibration_history: Execution, formal, browser, property, fuzz, simulation, and external-evidence routes are existing authoritative checks.
signals.known_failure_modes: Pass/fail warrants do not expose a comparable incremental metric such as hidden-test subsets, objective delta, or proof-obligation coverage.

interventions.name: stance_decay
interventions.replay_visible: derived from lineage and frozen config
interventions.calibration_history: Fast decay outperformed permanent stances in the prior live basin study.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Lineage count conflates admitted volume with useful progress and can differ across matched branches after treatment.

interventions.name: school_reseed_stance_rotation
interventions.calibration_history: Prior basin evidence favours rotation, but no matched causal estimate exists.
interventions.known_failure_modes: The existing helper creates a school-policy artifact which is accepted by default; it cannot be the pilot treatment because treatment would mutate ontology/status.

interventions.name: forced_crossover
interventions.calibration_history: Added to prevent stance-only rotation from echoing the same lineage; no isolated causal calibration found.
interventions.known_failure_modes: The current path depends on a reseed policy artifact, so the safe prompt-only part is not independently exposed.

interventions.name: complement_directive
interventions.replay_visible: config/intervention plus rendered prompt on LLM receipt
interventions.calibration_history: Reduced prompt echo but did not materially restore novelty in the prior basin study.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Can increase surface contrast without functional novelty; useful primarily as a negative control.

interventions.name: tail_weighted_selection
interventions.replay_visible: activation is logged by the response ladder; candidate typicality is in raw output
interventions.known_failure_modes: Typicality is model self-report, so treatment intensity can vary by model and may not correspond to functional diversity.

interventions.name: specification_injection
interventions.replay_visible: spec call and transmission score are logged
interventions.calibration_history: Existing Level-2 diversity mechanism; no isolated matched jolt calibration found.
interventions.known_failure_modes: It spends a separate LLM call and adds a second stochastic generator, contaminating equal-call and equal-token branch comparisons.

interventions.name: capture_stagnation_recruit
interventions.known_failure_modes: Combines four treatments at once and would contaminate every experimental arm unless disabled in branch schedulers.

interventions.name: capture_orbit_response
interventions.calibration_history: Based on basin findings but not prospectively calibrated.
interventions.known_failure_modes: School-enabled response creates an accepted policy artifact; school-free response bundles tail weighting and spec injection.

interventions.name: problem_liveness_queue
interventions.replay_visible: selected problem appears in cycle heartbeat; in-memory age is reconstructable from those heartbeats
interventions.calibration_history: Production starvation fix; not a matched jolt.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Problem opportunity and difficulty differ, so turnover can change the outcome surface rather than restore exploration.

interventions.name: successor_and_discrimination_spawning
interventions.calibration_history: Core scheduler mechanism; prior problem turnover correlated with novelty recovery.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Spawning is downstream of current status and can reveal treatment outcomes; creating new problems after randomisation would violate identical branch opportunity sets.

interventions.name: controller_cap_and_timeout_updates
interventions.known_failure_modes: It creates accepted controller artifacts and can differentially change completion caps after treatment-induced truncation; pilot branches must run with controller off.

interventions.name: temperature_profile
interventions.replay_visible: route/profile is manifest-bound; provider sampling remains nondeterministic
interventions.calibration_history: High temperature did not rescue the weak-model arm and coincided with hard orbiting.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Changes output validity and transport behaviour as well as diversity; provider seed support is not an exact replay guarantee.

interventions.name: problem_and_family_focus
interventions.calibration_history: Production pipeline and liveness tests exist; no jolt calibration found.
interventions.known_failure_modes: A focus change alters task opportunity; post-trigger outcome must remain tied to the frozen original problem.

interventions.name: research_external_evidence_injection
interventions.deterministic: retrieval no; registration/replay yes for frozen receipts
interventions.calibration_history: External evidence is an existing authoritative route, not a calibrated diversity jolt.
interventions.known_failure_modes: It changes the information set and can directly create accepted evidence/support; excluded from the pilot rather than relabelled as an attention-only treatment.

interventions.name: anti_relapse_neighbourhood_suppression
interventions.replay_visible: rendered prompt and artifact/status graph are logged
interventions.enabled_by_default: accepted-only neighbourhood naturally excludes refuted artifacts
interventions.calibration_history: No isolated temporary lineage-suppression calibration found.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Suppressing an entire lineage may remove useful non-refuted material and changes context length; current pack API does not expose an explicit suppression receipt.

interventions.name: representation_reset
interventions.deterministic: not currently implemented as a typed action
interventions.calibration_history: No repository implementation or calibration located.
interventions.known_failure_modes: A free-form reset can smuggle in answer content or evaluator information; any pilot form must use frozen task-class templates.

interventions.name: verifier_directed_challenge
interventions.deterministic: not currently implemented as a typed action
interventions.calibration_history: No repository implementation or calibration located.
interventions.known_failure_modes: Withheld-test details would leak the outcome; only predeclared public deterministic failure classes are eligible.

census_conclusions: There is no existing common replay receipt for incremental functional novelty or verifier-progress magnitude across workloads.
Existing school reseed and controller actions create accepted artifacts even though their intended effect is process or attention; they are unsuitable as status-neutral experimental treatments.
Existing automatic capture responses bundle treatments and must be held inactive in experimental branches to preserve a no-jolt control.
Specification injection spends an additional stochastic LLM call and cannot be silently included in equal-budget matched branches.
External evidence injection changes both the information set and the epistemic graph and is excluded from the smallest valid pilot.

Exact matched continuation is not guaranteed by copying state because provider outputs are nondeterministic; matching can block on state, model, prompt, budgets, and branch order but still requires replication.
Scheduler liveness ages, cooldowns, and pending sandbox availability are not all persisted in the root; a copied root is not yet a complete live-process checkpoint.
A causal comparison of adaptive versus fixed scheduling is a run-level policy experiment and is not identified by a single conditional matched-branch pilot.
